action.reasoning: Observation: Iteration 2 train.py crashed with CUDA error 'no kernel image available' on GPU, preventing any training. Analysis: The pre-installed PyTorch CUDA kernels are incompatible with the GPU architecture; network is disabled so we cannot install compatible PyTorch. The only viable path is CPU-only training. This will be slower but should work. Decision: Rewrite train.py to use device='cpu', run one quick training loop over all 10 tasks with small total_timesteps (20000 each, ~2 min per task) to produce valid checkpoints. Set CUDA_VISIBLE_DEVICES='' in command to disable GPU. Submit eval immediately to test end-to-end pipeline and get a baseline success_rate, which will be low but informative. Time used: ~20 min, well within budget. Later iterations can scale timesteps and improve architecture.
